hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on lowrank_soft_codebook_residual with K=128, EXPANSION_FACTOR=2, OPTIMIZER=signum, LR=3.0e-3 unchanged. Final reconstruction formula is x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m pi_m c_m + Σ_i alpha_i b_i, where U(Vx) is a static low-rank trunk library, {c_m} is a static soft-codebook library, and {b_i} is a static residual dictionary. This is 扩展兼容: LUTurbo can export it as trunk lookup + codebook lookup + one residual sparse lookup, all over fixed vector libraries. The single question for this probe is whether removing the second residual refinement stage keeps enough reconstruction quality to justify the lower encoder selection cost and likely lower runtime in the low-EF regime.
next_hypotheses: If FVU regresses materially versus the mainline while runtime savings are small, return to lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual next round and stop probing nearby parents.

If runtime improves meaningfully at only modest FVU loss, keep lowrank_soft_codebook_residual as a cost-efficient compatible branch on the Pareto frontier, but still return to the mainline next round per probe policy.
notes_to_memory: Compatibility: 扩展兼容. Export path is static trunk library + static codebook library + static residual dictionary; no dynamic decoder weights.
This is a matched architecture probe that changes only ARCHITECTURE and stays safely within the known EF=2 selection-cost budget table.
Probe intent is architectural value judgment only: test whether the second sparse stage in the current mainline is worth its extra encoder cost/runtime under LUTurbo constraints.
self_review: This probe is conservative because the family is already known and compatible, so upside is mostly in runtime-cost tradeoff rather than discovering a new best FVU. That makes it appropriate after the previous matched probe was policy-rejected: it isolates a single structural difference and should yield a clean comparable signal.
summary: Run a single matched architecture probe with ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_soft_codebook_residual while keeping K=128, EXPANSION_FACTOR=2, OPTIMIZER=signum, and LR=3.0e-3 fixed. The probe tests whether the simpler one-stage soft-codebook parent is still worth continuing as a lower-cost LUTurbo-compatible branch relative to the current two-stage mainline.
